close_reason: Arc<Client> is intentional for cross-task sharing
description:
secretx-aws-sm/src/lib.rs:39 stores 'client: Arc<aws_sdk_secretsmanager::Client>'. aws_sdk_secretsmanager::Client is internally Arc'd already, so the outer Arc is redundant for cloning purposes.

Why this is fine: the AwsSmBackend itself is held inside Arc<dyn SecretStore> by callers via inventory dispatch (factory returns Arc<...> at lib.rs:258, 266). The double-Arc costs one extra atomic refcount per construction (not per call) and gains uniformity with sister backends:
  - secretx-aws-kms/src/lib.rs:49 uses Arc<aws_sdk_kms::Client>
  - secretx-aws-ssm/src/lib.rs presumably the same

Defendable: the outer Arc is consistent with sister AWS backends; uniformity matters for code review.

Counterpart: see crate-secretsx-8bh8.6 for the opposing 'remove the outer Arc' opinion. Pick one, close the other.

Cross-reference: this mirrors the pair crate-secretsx-csz6 / crate-secretsx-vfxr for secretx-aws-kms.
title: secretx-aws-sm: Arc<aws_sdk_secretsmanager::Client> is intentional for cross-crate uniformity
